summary: Host-owned prompt for trust-, permission-, or identity-changing actions. The prompt keeps scope, owner, raw identifiers, consequences, and safe-local continuity visible before the user changes authority.

hook_records.notes: Reference review theme for trust prompts.
Forced-colors and high-contrast review keep trust meaning intact without hue-only signaling.
Permission prompts follow the shared appearance and token-overlay model.
Theme or contrast changes apply live while the prompt is open.

slots.description: Dialog or sheet container that traps focus until the prompt resolves.
State icon communicating trust or warning posture.
Prompt headline naming the capability or change under review.
Workspace, target, owner, and expiry summary.
Raw owner, publisher, or provider identifier shown in strict display mode.
Raw permission or capability identifier shown exactly when a compatibility or trust decision depends on it.
Plain-language list of what the action allows, what boundary changes, and what remains excluded.
Local-only or reduced-mode alternative when the user declines or the policy blocks widening.
Optional docs or policy detail route.
Explicit decline route that preserves a productive local path.
Safe reduced-mode continuation when the workflow supports it.

responsive_reflow_notes: Raw identifiers never collapse behind an expander; action labels or help affordances collapse first.
The safe-local continuity note stays above the action row in compact layouts so the alternative is visible before focus enters the buttons.
The prompt may become a sheet on narrow layouts, but focus order and action wording stay unchanged.

state_nodes.user_visible_summary: Owner or capability context is still being resolved and the prompt must not imply trust yet.
The user must review scope, owner, and consequences before widening authority.
The action can continue only in a reduced local or inspect-only mode.
Administrative policy prevents the requested authority change.
The user approved the action and the prompt is handing control to the owning surface or route.

transitions.user_visible_effect: The prompt replaces loading text with exact scope, identifiers, and consequences.
Approval records the decision and moves control to the owning route without hiding provenance.
The prompt narrows to the safe-local path and keeps the denied capability explicit.
The prompt disables approval and shows the governing policy source and next review path.
The prompt re-enables review once the policy posture allows it again.

slot_content_rules.notes: The title names the capability or trust change in direct language.
Scope summary includes exact dates or versions when expiry or deprecation is relevant.
Publisher, provider, or owner identifiers remain exact and reveal confusable or invisible-text issues.
Capability identifiers stay exact when support, policy, or docs flows need to match them verbatim.
The list says what the action enables, what it does not enable, and the retention or boundary impact when relevant.
The reduced-mode alternative stays visible even when the prompt blocks widening.
Actions must name the consequence, not a generic yes/no verb.
Deny keeps the productive local path explicit.
Continue-local is distinct from deny and from allow.

content_contract.blocked_and_degraded_copy_rule: Restricted or policy-blocked variants explicitly name what still works locally, what boundary stays closed, and how the user can recover or review the rule.
content_contract.empty_or_missing_data_rule: If owner or capability metadata cannot be resolved, the prompt stays in the loading state and disables trust-changing actions rather than guessing.

primary_navigation_keys.effect: Move to the next focusable action or help route inside the prompt.
Move to the previous focusable action or help route inside the prompt.

activation_keys.effect: Activate the focused action after the user reviews scope and consequence text.
activation_keys.when: When one of the prompt actions owns focus.

dismissal_keys.effect: Dismiss the prompt when a safe deny or continue-local path exists.
dismissal_keys.when: Only when dismissal does not skip a required acknowledgement.

interaction_contract.focus_return_rule: Closing or denying returns focus to the invoking control or the next safe productive surface, never to a hidden or destroyed element.
interaction_contract.pointer_behavior_note: Pointer interaction mirrors the keyboard route exactly; no consequence explanation or raw identifier is hidden behind hover.

accessibility_contract.role_description: Trust- or permission-changing dialog or sheet.
accessibility_contract.accessible_name_rule: The accessible name combines the prompt title and the capability or scope phrase so the user hears what is being decided immediately.
accessibility_contract.accessible_description_rule: The accessible description appends owner identifier, scope summary, consequences, safe-local note, and any policy-block reason in stable order.

announcement_rules.message_rule: Initial focus lands on the safest sensible action while the title and scope summary remain discoverable from the dialog root.
Policy denial announces the governing source and the remaining safe-local path without moving focus unexpectedly.
The prompt announces that a reduced local path is available and keeps the button in the normal tab order.

accessibility_contract.high_contrast_behavior: Header icon, focus ring, and action emphasis remain distinct in both high-contrast themes, and raw identifier blocks keep border separation from explanatory text.
accessibility_contract.forced_colors_behavior: Forced-colors mode preserves icon, border, and text hierarchy while flattening decorative fills; the prompt still exposes which action is safest.
accessibility_contract.blocked_and_degraded_behavior: Policy-blocked and restricted variants keep consequences, safe-local continuity, and recovery routes in the accessibility tree instead of requiring visual comparison.

semantic_token_bindings.notes: Icon treatment follows the prompt state and never acts alone.
Reduced-mode continuity stays visually separated from the main consequence list.
Blocked-state copy remains readable and subordinate to the title.
Final approved handoff still preserves focus visibility during the route transition.

density_variant_bindings.layout_effect: Action row tightens, but raw identifiers remain full-width and readable.
Reference density for trust and permission review.
More vertical separation is allowed for accessibility or long localized strings without changing action order.

motion_bindings.reduced_motion_behavior: Enter and exit collapse to instant transitions; focus and announcement order stay unchanged.

state_visualization_bindings.notes: Review-required is cautionary but not yet blocked.
Restricted path keeps the safe-local action and explanation co-located.
Policy denial stays separate from restricted local mode.

asset_slots.notes: Trust and policy prompts use the same host-owned icon slot.
Trust-critical prompts do not use decorative illustration.
Help or policy routes may use a directional link icon.

localization_contract.text_expansion_strategy: Title and scope wrap before the action row reflows. Raw identifiers preserve exact bytes and may horizontal-scroll or line-break only at safe separators.
localization_contract.bidi_behavior: Owner and capability identifiers are bidi-isolated and never mirrored; surrounding explanatory copy follows the active locale direction.
localization_contract.formatting_behavior: Expiry, deadline, and version text use exact locale-aware dates or versions instead of vague relative phrasing on durable or trust-critical prompts.
localization_contract.source_language_fallback_behavior: If only source-language publisher or policy labels exist, the prompt keeps those exact labels and adds translated framing rather than silently substituting approximate copy.
localization_contract.notes: This component must survive pseudoloc expansion, mixed-direction identifiers, and confusable-character review without collapsing the safe-local path.

allowed_extension_variations: An extension may request one extension-specific docs link or policy-details link.
An extension may pass through a provider-specific identifier as raw metadata when the host prompt exposes it exactly.
extension_guidance.required_gap_disclosure: UI-bearing extensions should invoke the host-owned trust prompt; if they cannot, they must hand off before authority changes rather than shipping a look-alike prompt with weaker disclosure.

substitution_and_deprecation.unsupported_package_behavior: If a theme or extension package cannot express the host trust tokens faithfully, the prompt falls back to the host-owned trust palette and keeps the mismatch visible in docs/help or appearance review.
